citations.narrative:
A rabbit enclosure housing a single rabbit contains an excessive buildup of feces. In one corner, a mound of excreta
approximately five inches high has accumulated above the wire mesh flooring.
Failure to remove accumulations of excreta pose significant health risks by promoting the spread of disease, producing
strong and unpleasant odors, and attracting pests.
Primary enclosures for rabbits shall be kept reasonably free of excreta, hair, cobwebs, and other debris by periodic
cleaning. Correct this day, April 7, 2026, forward.
This inspection and exit interview were conducted with the facility representative.
n

narrative:
3.56(a)(1) 
   
Sanitation. 
A rabbit enclosure housing a single rabbit contains an excessive buildup of feces. In one corner, a mound of excreta 
approximately five inches high has accumulated above the wire mesh flooring. 
 
Failure to remove accumulations of excreta pose significant health risks by promoting the spread of disease, producing 
strong and unpleasant odors, and attracting pests. 
 
Primary enclosures for rabbits shall be kept reasonably free of excreta, hair, cobwebs, and other debris by periodic 
cleaning. Correct this day, April 7, 2026, forward. 
 
This inspection and exit interview were conducted with the facility representative.
